modules.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder

ToolBuilder class which provides flexibility to create tools
for Agent.

classes.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.__init__
documentation: Initializes the builder and resets all attributes.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.reset
documentation:
Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all attributes.
This is called after building a tool to prepare the builder
for the next tool creation.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_name
documentation:
Sets the name of the tool.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    The name that will identify the tool.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_function
documentation:
Sets the function for the tool.

Parameters
----------
function : Callable
    The function that the tool will execute when invoked.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_coroutine
documentation:
Sets the coroutine for the tool.

Parameters
----------
coroutine : Callable
    The asynchronous coroutine that the tool will execute.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_hint
documentation:
Sets the hint for the tool in case of exception.
The hint guides the agent to reiterate in case of exception
of tool.

Parameters
----------
coroutine : Callable
    The asynchronous coroutine that the tool will execute.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_description
documentation:
Sets the description for the tool.

Parameters
----------
description : str
    A short description of what the tool does.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_schema
documentation:
Sets the schema that defines the input structure for the tool.

Parameters
----------
schema : object
    The schema that defines the tool's input parameters.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_max_iterations
documentation:
Sets the maximum iterations for the tool.

Parameters
----------
max_iterations : int
    Number of iterations the tool could be called maximum.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.validate
documentation:
Validates that all necessary components (name, function, schema,
and description) are set before building the tool.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If any required attribute is not set before building.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.build
documentation:
Constructs and returns the tool using the provided configuration.

Returns
-------
tool : StructuredTool
    The tool that was constructed based on the configured parameters.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder._add_exception_handling
documentation: Adds decorator with given hint for exception handling of tool.

A builder class for constructing tools that agents can use.
Tools require essential components like a function, name, schema,
and description to be set before being built. This class provides
methods to configure and build these tools.

Attributes
----------
name : str
    The name of the tool.
function : Callable
    The main function that the tool will execute.
coroutine : Callable, optional
    The coroutine that the tool will execute, if applicable.
return_direct : bool
    Whether the tool should return the result directly.
    Default is False.
description : str
    A description of what the tool does.
schema : object
    The schema that defines the structure of the tool's input.

modules.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder
documentation: AgentBuilder to build agents.

classes.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.__init__
documentation: Initialize the builder and reset all attributes to default values.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.reset
documentation:
Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all previously set configurations.
This method is called after the agent is built to ensure the builder can be reused.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_llm
documentation:
Sets the language model (LLM) for the agent.

Parameters
----------
llm : Any
    The language model that the agent will use for generating responses.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.add_tool
documentation:
Adds a tool to the agent's toolset, allowing the agent to perform specific tasks.

Parameters
----------
tool : Any
    The tool to be added to the agent's capabilities.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_role
documentation:
Sets the role that the agent will assume during interactions.

Parameters
----------
role : str
    The role or persona that the agent will embody (e.g., assistant, advisor).

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_action_control
documentation:
Sets the action control mechanism that manages how the agent processes and
executes actions.

Parameters
----------
action_control : object
    The object responsible for handling the agent's action control (e.g., an output parser).

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_format_intermediate_steps
documentation:
Sets the function that formats intermediate steps during the agent's execution.

Parameters
----------
format_intermediate_steps : object
    A callable that formats intermediate steps in the agent's task execution.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.add_delegation_agent
documentation:
Adds another agent to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

Parameters
----------
delegation_agent : object
    An agent to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_goal
documentation:
Sets the primary goal or objective that the agent should aim to achieve.

Parameters
----------
goal : str
    The primary purpose or objective assigned to the agent.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.configure_memory
documentation:
Configures the memory object to enable memory-based functionality for the agent.

Parameters
----------
memory : object
    The memory configuration to be used by the agent.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.get_instructions_json
documentation:
Generates the agent's building instructions as a JSON object.

Returns
-------
dict
    The agent's configuration and instructions in JSON format.

Raises
------
NotImplementedError
    This method is not yet implemented.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.render_instructions_json
documentation:
Renders the agent's instructions back into the builder, possibly to re-instantiate
or rebuild an agent from a JSON configuration.

Returns
-------
dict
    The agent's configuration rendered as JSON.

Raises
------
NotImplementedError
    This method is not yet implemented.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.validate
documentation:
Validates that all necessary components for building the agent are properly set,
such as LLM, goal, action control, and formatting functions.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If any required component is missing or not configured.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.build
documentation:
Constructs and returns the fully built agent based on the configured parameters.

The agent is built with the specified LLM, tools, goal, role, action control,
and delegation agents.

Returns
-------
Agent
    The fully constructed agent with the specified configuration.

A builder class for constructing agents that interact with tools, memory, goals,
and Language Learning Models (LLMs). This class follows a builder pattern,
providing methods to configure various components of the agent and ensure
the agent is fully customizable.

The agent can be configured with:
- LLMs (Language Learning Models)
- Tools (for extended capabilities)
- A goal or objective (to guide the agent's purpose)
- Memory (for persistent knowledge or interaction history)
- Action control mechanisms (to manage agent behavior)
- Delegation agents (for task delegation)

Attributes
----------
agent : Agent
    The final agent object that is built by this builder.
llm : Any
    The language model (e.g., GPT) that the agent will use for responses.
tools : list
    A list of tools that the agent can use to perform specific tasks.
goal : str
    The primary goal or objective of the agent.
role : str
    The role assigned to the agent (e.g., assistant, expert).
action_control : OpenAIToolsAgentOutputParser
    The action control mechanism to manage how the agent interprets
    and executes actions.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to format intermediate steps during the agent's execution process.
memory_object : object
    The memory configuration used to enable memory-based capabilities for the agent.
delegating_agents : list
    A list of other agents to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

Methods
-------
reset()
    Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all configurations.
set_llm(llm)
    Sets the language model for the agent.
add_tool(tool)
    Adds a tool to the agent's toolset.
set_role(role)
    Sets the role for the agent.
set_action_control(action_control)
    Sets the action control mechanism for the agent.
set_format_intermediate_steps(format_intermediate_steps)
    Sets the format function for intermediate steps in agent execution.
add_delegation_agent(delegation_agent)
    Adds a delegation agent to the agent's list of delegation agents.
set_goal(goal)
    Sets the primary goal for the agent.
configure_memory(memory)
    Configures memory for the agent.
get_instructions_json()
    Generates the agent's instructions as a JSON object (not implemented).
render_instructions_json()
    Renders agent instructions from JSON to rebuild the agent (not implemented).
validate()
    Validates all necessary components before building the agent.
build()
    Builds and returns the fully configured agent.

modules.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.utils.raise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
This module provides a utility decorator for handling exceptions in
synchronous functions within the context of a tool-based architecture.

When an synchronous function that is decorated with this utility fails,
it raises a `ToolException`. This captures and reports details about the
failure to the agent, allowing the agent's execution to continue without
abrupt interruptions.

The decorator can be customized with a hint message that provides additional
context about the potential issue, helping to diagnose problems effectively.

functions.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.utils.raise_tool_exception_on_fail.raise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
Implements a wrapper function that raises ToolException.
This approach ensures that any function failure is captured and
reported as an observation to the agent, instead of causing the
entire execution process to terminate.

Parameters
----------
hint : str
    Custom message, used to give a hint to the agent of what could be wrong.

Returns
------
decorator : callable
    A decorator function that can be applied to other functions.

modules.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.utils.araise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
This module provides a utility decorator for handling exceptions in
asynchronous functions within the context of a tool-based architecture.

When an asynchronous function that is decorated with this utility fails,
it raises a `ToolException`. This captures and reports details about the
failure to the agent, allowing the agent's execution to continue without
abrupt interruptions.

The decorator can be customized with a hint message that provides additional
context about the potential issue, helping to diagnose problems effectively.

functions.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.utils.araise_tool_exception_on_fail.araise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
Implements a wrapper function that raises ToolException.
This approach ensures that any function failure is captured and
reported as an observation to the agent, instead of causing the
entire execution process to terminate.

Parameters
----------
hint : str
    Custom message, used to give a hint to the agent of what could be wrong.

Returns
------
decorator : callable
    A decorator function that can be applied to other functions.

classes.name: build.lib.agent_builder.builders.base_builder.BaseBuilder

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.builders.base_builder.BaseBuilder.reset
documentation:
Resets the builder to its initial state.
Must be implemented by concrete builders.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.builders.base_builder.BaseBuilder.build
documentation:
Abstract method to finalize and build the component (agent, tool, etc.).
Must be implemented by concrete builders.

Abstract base class for building components like agents or tools.
This class defines the basic structure for configuring and building
various components.

modules.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent
documentation:
An Agent class used for stateful agent interaction with tools and
LLMs (Language Learning Models).

classes.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the stateful agent with its configuration,
including tools, role, memory, and task delegation agents.

Parameters
----------
llm : Any
    The language model that the agent will use.
goal : str
    The main purpose or objective of the agent.
action_control : Any
    The output parser to control the agent's actions.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to format intermediate steps for logging.
tools : list, optional
    A list of tools available to the agent. Default is an empty list.
role : str, optional
    The role assigned to the agent. Default is an empty string.
max_iterations : int, optional
    The maximum number of iterations the agent can take in a
    single task. Default is 15.
return_intermediate_steps : bool, optional
    A flag to indicate whether intermediate steps should be
    returned in the response. Default is True.
delegating_agents : list, optional
    A list of agents to which tasks can be delegated. Default
    is an empty list.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.build_chain
documentation:
Builds a LangChain-based agent chain, with the defined tools,
for executing tasks. The chain incorporates the tools, prompt,
and LLM into a single pipeline.

Returns
-------
AgentExecutor
    The agent's executor object, which is ready to process
    tasks.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.get_tools
documentation:
Abstract method that should be implemented by subclasses
to create a list of tools assigned to the agent.

Returns
-------
list
    A list of tools assigned to the agent.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.invoke
documentation:
Invoke the agent synchronously with the given
input and chat history.

Parameters:
-----------
input : str
    The input string to be processed by the agent.
    This is added as the user message in executor chain.

chat_history : list
    The chat history, provided as a list, to be
    considered by the agent. The schema
    has user, assistant messages.

Returns:
--------
response : dict
    The response from the agent, which includes
    the agent's action sequence and any other relevant data.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.ainvoke
documentation:
Invoke the agent asynchronously with the given
input and chat history.

Parameters:
-----------
input : str
    The input string to be processed by the agent.
    This is added as the user message in executor chain.

chat_history : list
    The chat history, provided as a list, to be
    considered by the agent. The schema
    has user, assistant messages.

Returns:
--------
response : dict
    The response from the agent, which includes
    the agent's action sequence and any other relevant data.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.astream
documentation:
Stream agent's intermediate steps asynchronously, processing
input and chat history step by step.

Parameters
----------
input : str
    The user input message to be processed by the agent.
chat_history : list
    The chat history to be considered by the agent.

Yields
------
str
    The agent's action, intermediate step, or final result in
    a formatted string.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.stream
documentation:
Stream the agent's response using synchronous yield for each
intermediate step, including actions, steps, and final output.

Parameters
----------
input : str
    The user input message to be processed by the agent.
chat_history : list
    The chat history to be considered by the agent.

Yields
------
str
    The agent's action, intermediate step, or final result in
    a formatted string.

methods.name: build.lib.build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent._save_agent_action_sequence
documentation:
Save the agent's action sequence after processing the response.
It processes the intermediate steps and formats them into a
readable action sequence.

Parameters
----------
response : dict
    The response from the agent, containing the
    intermediate_steps.

Returns
-------
str
    The formatted action sequence.

A stateful agent designed for interacting with LLMs and performing
tasks with a toolset. The agent retains chat history, and its
behavior is shaped by the user's input, goal, and previous interactions.

Attributes
----------
llm : object
    The base chat model from LangChain.
user_name : str
    The user who is interacting with the agent.
action_control : MultiActionAgentOutputParser
    The class responsible for parsing the output of an LLM call.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to convert (AgentAction, tool output) tuples into
    FunctionMessages for further processing.
goal : str
    The main purpose or objective of the agent.
return_intermediate_steps : bool
    A flag to determine whether intermediate steps should be
    included in the response. Default is True.
action_sequence : str
    A formatted string representing actions performed by the agent.
    Example: "Step 1: Used `get-temperature` tool with `Mumbai`
    as input."
agent : AgentExecutor
    The AgentExecutor, inheriting from LangChain's AgentExecutor.
role : str
    The role of the agent in the interaction.
delegation_agents : list
    A list of subsequent agents to which tasks can be delegated.

The agent is built with the specified LLM, tools, goal, role, action control,
and delegation agents.

    The fully constructed agent with the specified configuration.

A builder class for constructing agents that interact with tools, memory, goals,
and Language Learning Models (LLMs). This class follows a builder pattern,
providing methods to configure various components of the agent and ensure
the agent is fully customizable.

The agent can be configured with:
- LLMs (Language Learning Models)
- Tools (for extended capabilities)
- A goal or objective (to guide the agent's purpose)
- Memory (for persistent knowledge or interaction history)
- Action control mechanisms (to manage agent behavior)
- Delegation agents (for task delegation)

Attributes
----------
agent : Agent
    The final agent object that is built by this builder.
llm : Any
    The language model (e.g., GPT) that the agent will use for responses.
tools : list
    A list of tools that the agent can use to perform specific tasks.
goal : str
    The primary goal or objective of the agent.
role : str
    The role assigned to the agent (e.g., assistant, expert).
action_control : OpenAIToolsAgentOutputParser
    The action control mechanism to manage how the agent interprets
    and executes actions.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to format intermediate steps during the agent's execution process.
memory_object : object
    The memory configuration used to enable memory-based capabilities for the agent.
delegating_agents : list
    A list of other agents to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

Methods
-------
reset()
    Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all configurations.
set_llm(llm)
    Sets the language model for the agent.
add_tool(tool)
    Adds a tool to the agent's toolset.
set_role(role)
    Sets the role for the agent.
set_action_control(action_control)
    Sets the action control mechanism for the agent.
set_format_intermediate_steps(format_intermediate_steps)
    Sets the format function for intermediate steps in agent execution.
add_delegation_agent(delegation_agent)
    Adds a delegation agent to the agent's list of delegation agents.
set_goal(goal)
    Sets the primary goal for the agent.
configure_memory(memory)
    Configures memory for the agent.
get_instructions_json()
    Generates the agent's instructions as a JSON object (not implemented).
render_instructions_json()
    Renders agent instructions from JSON to rebuild the agent (not implemented).
validate()
    Validates all necessary components before building the agent.
build()
    Builds and returns the fully configured agent.

modules.name: build.lib.agent_builder.utils.raise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
This module provides a utility decorator for handling exceptions in
synchronous functions within the context of a tool-based architecture.

When an synchronous function that is decorated with this utility fails,
it raises a `ToolException`. This captures and reports details about the
failure to the agent, allowing the agent's execution to continue without
abrupt interruptions.

The decorator can be customized with a hint message that provides additional
context about the potential issue, helping to diagnose problems effectively.

functions.name: build.lib.agent_builder.utils.raise_tool_exception_on_fail.raise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
Implements a wrapper function that raises ToolException.
This approach ensures that any function failure is captured and
reported as an observation to the agent, instead of causing the
entire execution process to terminate.

Parameters
----------
hint : str
    Custom message, used to give a hint to the agent of what could be wrong.

Returns
------
decorator : callable
    A decorator function that can be applied to other functions.

modules.name: build.lib.agent_builder.utils.araise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
This module provides a utility decorator for handling exceptions in
asynchronous functions within the context of a tool-based architecture.

When an asynchronous function that is decorated with this utility fails,
it raises a `ToolException`. This captures and reports details about the
failure to the agent, allowing the agent's execution to continue without
abrupt interruptions.

The decorator can be customized with a hint message that provides additional
context about the potential issue, helping to diagnose problems effectively.

functions.name: build.lib.agent_builder.utils.araise_tool_exception_on_fail.araise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
Implements a wrapper function that raises ToolException.
This approach ensures that any function failure is captured and
reported as an observation to the agent, instead of causing the
entire execution process to terminate.

Parameters
----------
hint : str
    Custom message, used to give a hint to the agent of what could be wrong.

Returns
------
decorator : callable
    A decorator function that can be applied to other functions.

classes.name: agent_builder.builders.base_builder.BaseBuilder

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.base_builder.BaseBuilder.reset
documentation:
Resets the builder to its initial state.
Must be implemented by concrete builders.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.base_builder.BaseBuilder.build
documentation:
Abstract method to finalize and build the component (agent, tool, etc.).
Must be implemented by concrete builders.

Abstract base class for building components like agents or tools.
This class defines the basic structure for configuring and building
various components.

modules.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent
documentation:
An Agent class used for stateful agent interaction with tools and
LLMs (Language Learning Models).

classes.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the stateful agent with its configuration,
including tools, role, memory, and task delegation agents.

Parameters
----------
llm : Any
    The language model that the agent will use.
goal : str
    The main purpose or objective of the agent.
action_control : Any
    The output parser to control the agent's actions.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to format intermediate steps for logging.
tools : list, optional
    A list of tools available to the agent. Default is an empty list.
role : str, optional
    The role assigned to the agent. Default is an empty string.
max_iterations : int, optional
    The maximum number of iterations the agent can take in a
    single task. Default is 15.
return_intermediate_steps : bool, optional
    A flag to indicate whether intermediate steps should be
    returned in the response. Default is True.
delegating_agents : list, optional
    A list of agents to which tasks can be delegated. Default
    is an empty list.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.build_chain
documentation:
Builds a LangChain-based agent chain, with the defined tools,
for executing tasks. The chain incorporates the tools, prompt,
and LLM into a single pipeline.

Returns
-------
AgentExecutor
    The agent's executor object, which is ready to process
    tasks.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.get_tools
documentation:
Abstract method that should be implemented by subclasses
to create a list of tools assigned to the agent.

Returns
-------
list
    A list of tools assigned to the agent.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.invoke
documentation:
Invoke the agent synchronously with the given
input and chat history.

Parameters:
-----------
input : str
    The input string to be processed by the agent.
    This is added as the user message in executor chain.

chat_history : list
    The chat history, provided as a list, to be
    considered by the agent. The schema
    has user, assistant messages.

Returns:
--------
response : dict
    The response from the agent, which includes
    the agent's action sequence and any other relevant data.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.ainvoke
documentation:
Invoke the agent asynchronously with the given
input and chat history.

Parameters:
-----------
input : str
    The input string to be processed by the agent.
    This is added as the user message in executor chain.

chat_history : list
    The chat history, provided as a list, to be
    considered by the agent. The schema
    has user, assistant messages.

Returns:
--------
response : dict
    The response from the agent, which includes
    the agent's action sequence and any other relevant data.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.astream
documentation:
Stream agent's intermediate steps asynchronously, processing
input and chat history step by step.

Parameters
----------
input : str
    The user input message to be processed by the agent.
chat_history : list
    The chat history to be considered by the agent.

Yields
------
str
    The agent's action, intermediate step, or final result in
    a formatted string.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent.stream
documentation:
Stream the agent's response using synchronous yield for each
intermediate step, including actions, steps, and final output.

Parameters
----------
input : str
    The user input message to be processed by the agent.
chat_history : list
    The chat history to be considered by the agent.

Yields
------
str
    The agent's action, intermediate step, or final result in
    a formatted string.

methods.name: build.lib.agent_builder.agents.agent.Agent._save_agent_action_sequence
documentation:
Save the agent's action sequence after processing the response.
It processes the intermediate steps and formats them into a
readable action sequence.

Parameters
----------
response : dict
    The response from the agent, containing the
    intermediate_steps.

Returns
-------
str
    The formatted action sequence.

A stateful agent designed for interacting with LLMs and performing
tasks with a toolset. The agent retains chat history, and its
behavior is shaped by the user's input, goal, and previous interactions.

Attributes
----------
llm : object
    The base chat model from LangChain.
user_name : str
    The user who is interacting with the agent.
action_control : MultiActionAgentOutputParser
    The class responsible for parsing the output of an LLM call.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to convert (AgentAction, tool output) tuples into
    FunctionMessages for further processing.
goal : str
    The main purpose or objective of the agent.
return_intermediate_steps : bool
    A flag to determine whether intermediate steps should be
    included in the response. Default is True.
action_sequence : str
    A formatted string representing actions performed by the agent.
    Example: "Step 1: Used `get-temperature` tool with `Mumbai`
    as input."
agent : AgentExecutor
    The AgentExecutor, inheriting from LangChain's AgentExecutor.
role : str
    The role of the agent in the interaction.
delegation_agents : list
    A list of subsequent agents to which tasks can be delegated.

modules.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder
documentation:
ToolBuilder class which provides flexibility to create tools
for Agent.

classes.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.__init__
documentation: Initializes the builder and resets all attributes.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.reset
documentation:
Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all attributes.
This is called after building a tool to prepare the builder
for the next tool creation.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_name
documentation:
Sets the name of the tool.

Parameters
----------
name : str

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_function
documentation:
Sets the function for the tool.

Parameters
----------

documentation:
Sets the coroutine for the tool.

Parameters

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_hint
documentation:
Sets the hint for the tool in case of exception.
The hint guides the agent to reiterate in case of exception
of tool.

Parameters
----------
coroutine : Callable
    The asynchronous coroutine that the tool will execute.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_description
documentation:
Sets the description for the tool.

Parameters
----------
description : str
    A short description of what the tool does.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_schema
documentation:
Sets the schema that defines the input structure for the tool.

Parameters
----------
schema : object
    The schema that defines the tool's input parameters.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.set_max_iterations
documentation:
Sets the maximum iterations for the tool.

Parameters
----------
max_iterations : int
    Number of iterations the tool could be called maximum.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.validate
documentation:
Validates that all necessary components (name, function, schema,
and description) are set before building the tool.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If any required attribute is not set before building.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder.build
documentation:
Constructs and returns the tool using the provided configuration.

Returns
-------
tool : StructuredTool
    The tool that was constructed based on the configured parameters.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.tool_builder.ToolBuilder._add_exception_handling
documentation: Adds decorator with given hint for exception handling of tool.

A builder class for constructing tools that agents can use.
Tools require essential components like a function, name, schema,
and description to be set before being built. This class provides
methods to configure and build these tools.

Attributes
----------
name : str
    The name of the tool.
function : Callable
    The main function that the tool will execute.
coroutine : Callable, optional
    The coroutine that the tool will execute, if applicable.
return_direct : bool
    Whether the tool should return the result directly.
    Default is False.
description : str
    A description of what the tool does.
schema : object
    The schema that defines the structure of the tool's input.

modules.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder
documentation: AgentBuilder to build agents.

classes.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.__init__
documentation: Initialize the builder and reset all attributes to default values.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.reset
documentation:
Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all previously set configurations.
This method is called after the agent is built to ensure the builder can be reused.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_llm
documentation:
Sets the language model (LLM) for the agent.

Parameters
----------
llm : Any
    The language model that the agent will use for generating responses.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.add_tool
documentation:
Adds a tool to the agent's toolset, allowing the agent to perform specific tasks.

Parameters
----------
tool : Any
    The tool to be added to the agent's capabilities.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_role
documentation:
Sets the role that the agent will assume during interactions.

Parameters
----------
role : str
    The role or persona that the agent will embody (e.g., assistant, advisor).

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_action_control
documentation:
Sets the action control mechanism that manages how the agent processes and
executes actions.

Parameters
----------
action_control : object
    The object responsible for handling the agent's action control (e.g., an output parser).

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_format_intermediate_steps
documentation:
Sets the function that formats intermediate steps during the agent's execution.

Parameters
----------
format_intermediate_steps : object
    A callable that formats intermediate steps in the agent's task execution.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.add_delegation_agent
documentation:
Adds another agent to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

Parameters
----------
delegation_agent : object
    An agent to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.set_goal
documentation:
Sets the primary goal or objective that the agent should aim to achieve.

Parameters
----------
goal : str
    The primary purpose or objective assigned to the agent.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.configure_memory
documentation:
Configures the memory object to enable memory-based functionality for the agent.

Parameters
----------
memory : object
    The memory configuration to be used by the agent.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.get_instructions_json
documentation:
Generates the agent's building instructions as a JSON object.

Returns
-------
dict
    The agent's configuration and instructions in JSON format.

Raises
------
NotImplementedError
    This method is not yet implemented.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.render_instructions_json
documentation:
Renders the agent's instructions back into the builder, possibly to re-instantiate
or rebuild an agent from a JSON configuration.

Returns
-------
dict
    The agent's configuration rendered as JSON.

Raises
------
NotImplementedError
    This method is not yet implemented.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.validate
documentation:
Validates that all necessary components for building the agent are properly set,
such as LLM, goal, action control, and formatting functions.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If any required component is missing or not configured.

methods.name: agent_builder.builders.agent_builder.AgentBuilder.build
documentation:
Constructs and returns the fully built agent based on the configured parameters.

The agent is built with the specified LLM, tools, goal, role, action control,
and delegation agents.

Returns
-------
Agent
    The fully constructed agent with the specified configuration.

A builder class for constructing agents that interact with tools, memory, goals,
and Language Learning Models (LLMs). This class follows a builder pattern,
providing methods to configure various components of the agent and ensure
the agent is fully customizable.

The agent can be configured with:
- LLMs (Language Learning Models)
- Tools (for extended capabilities)
- A goal or objective (to guide the agent's purpose)
- Memory (for persistent knowledge or interaction history)
- Action control mechanisms (to manage agent behavior)
- Delegation agents (for task delegation)

Attributes
----------
agent : Agent
    The final agent object that is built by this builder.
llm : Any
    The language model (e.g., GPT) that the agent will use for responses.
tools : list
    A list of tools that the agent can use to perform specific tasks.
goal : str
    The primary goal or objective of the agent.
role : str
    The role assigned to the agent (e.g., assistant, expert).
action_control : OpenAIToolsAgentOutputParser
    The action control mechanism to manage how the agent interprets
    and executes actions.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to format intermediate steps during the agent's execution process.
memory_object : object
    The memory configuration used to enable memory-based capabilities for the agent.
delegating_agents : list
    A list of other agents to which tasks can be delegated by the primary agent.

Methods
-------
reset()
    Resets the builder to its initial state, clearing all configurations.
set_llm(llm)
    Sets the language model for the agent.
add_tool(tool)
    Adds a tool to the agent's toolset.
set_role(role)
    Sets the role for the agent.
set_action_control(action_control)
    Sets the action control mechanism for the agent.
set_format_intermediate_steps(format_intermediate_steps)
    Sets the format function for intermediate steps in agent execution.
add_delegation_agent(delegation_agent)
    Adds a delegation agent to the agent's list of delegation agents.
set_goal(goal)
    Sets the primary goal for the agent.
configure_memory(memory)
    Configures memory for the agent.
get_instructions_json()
    Generates the agent's instructions as a JSON object (not implemented).
render_instructions_json()
    Renders agent instructions from JSON to rebuild the agent (not implemented).
validate()
    Validates all necessary components before building the agent.
build()
    Builds and returns the fully configured agent.

modules.name: agent_builder.utils.raise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
This module provides a utility decorator for handling exceptions in
synchronous functions within the context of a tool-based architecture.

When an synchronous function that is decorated with this utility fails,
it raises a `ToolException`. This captures and reports details about the
failure to the agent, allowing the agent's execution to continue without
abrupt interruptions.

The decorator can be customized with a hint message that provides additional
context about the potential issue, helping to diagnose problems effectively.

functions.name: agent_builder.utils.raise_tool_exception_on_fail.raise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
Implements a wrapper function that raises ToolException.
This approach ensures that any function failure is captured and
reported as an observation to the agent, instead of causing the
entire execution process to terminate.

Parameters
----------
hint : str
    Custom message, used to give a hint to the agent of what could be wrong.

Returns
------
decorator : callable
    A decorator function that can be applied to other functions.

modules.name: agent_builder.utils.araise_tool_exception_on_fail
documentation:
This module provides a utility decorator for handling exceptions in
asynchronous functions within the context of a tool-based architecture.

When an asynchronous function that is decorated with this utility fails,
it raises a `ToolException`. This captures and reports details about the

abrupt interruptions.

The decorator can be customized with a hint message that provides additional
context about the potential issue, helping to diagnose problems effectively.

Implements a wrapper function that raises ToolException.

Parameters

hint : str
    Custom message, used to give a hint to the agent of what could be wrong.

Returns
------
decorator : callable
    A decorator function that can be applied to other functions.

A stateful agent designed for interacting with LLMs and performing
tasks with a toolset. The agent retains chat history, and its
behavior is shaped by the user's input, goal, and previous interactions.

Attributes
----------
llm : object
    The base chat model from LangChain.
user_name : str
    The user who is interacting with the agent.
action_control : MultiActionAgentOutputParser
    The class responsible for parsing the output of an LLM call.
format_intermediate_steps : Callable
    A function to convert (AgentAction, tool output) tuples into
    FunctionMessages for further processing.
goal : str
    The main purpose or objective of the agent.
return_intermediate_steps : bool
    A flag to determine whether intermediate steps should be
    included in the response. Default is True.
action_sequence : str
    A formatted string representing actions performed by the agent.
    Example: "Step 1: Used `get-temperature` tool with `Mumbai`
    as input."
agent : AgentExecutor
    The AgentExecutor, inheriting from LangChain's AgentExecutor.
role : str
    The role of the agent in the interaction.
delegation_agents : list
    A list of subsequent agents to which tasks can be delegated.
